subtasks.description: Evaluate contractor temporary access request against data access policies. Determine if contractor role permits customer database backup downloads and check data classification/export restrictions.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier: The query explicitly requests policy evaluation for access control and compliance. This is a governance decision requiring assessment against multiple policy dimensions (role-based access, data sensitivity, export restrictions, contractor agreement terms). Mid tier handles multi-policy reasoning with exceptions (e.g., temporary access scope, backup download restrictions, data classification). No additional subtasks needed—the policy evaluation is the core requirement here. No cross-recon, no tool orchestration, no SQL/query generation is needed since this is purely a policy governance question.

output.reason: Contractor role lacks DBA privileges required for backup access (POL-701), full database download violates de-identification and no-raw-export rule for contractors (POL-702), and no evidence of PM approval or 7-day notice (POL-703).
